_comment: Web-extracted priced benefit terms for the 5 largest seed employers. Extracted from employer-published benefits pages / total-rewards PDFs (and SEC 11-K filings) in 2026-07. Each term keeps its source_url + verbatim snippet. confidence: 'reported' = explicit number from an employer-published/authoritative source; 'inferred' = summarized or third-party. These are CANDIDATE values for the valuation engine; crowdsourcing confirms/corrects them. link_plan_type attaches a term to that employer's matching Form 5500 plan when applicable.

terms.value_text: Aerospace matches 100% of employee pre-tax/Roth deferrals up to a maximum of 3% of match-eligible compensation (non-AERP1 participants).
terms.source_snippet: For eligible Participants, Aerospace will match of 100% your Deferral Contributions up to a maximum of 3% of your match-eligible Compensation.
terms.notes: Dollar-for-dollar to 3%. AERP1 defined-benefit participants were not eligible for the 401(k) match/nonelective while accruing pension; eligible beginning Oct 1, 2023.

terms.value_text: Fixed nonelective (non-matching) employer contribution based on years of service: 5% (<5 yrs), 7% (5-24 yrs), 9% (25+ yrs). Combined with the 3% match this yields the total company-paid benefit of 8% / 10% / 12% cited in the total-rewards guide.
terms.source_snippet: Years of Service ... Nonelective Contribution: Less than 5 years 5%; 5-24 years 7%; 25+ years 9%
terms.notes: Total company-paid benefit = 8%/10%/12% of eligible comp (match + nonelective) per official Benefits Highlights. Non-AERP1 participants only.

terms.value_text: Immediate — participants are always 100% vested in all contributions.
terms.source_snippet: You are always 100% vested in your Plan account.
terms.notes: Benefits Highlights: 'immediately eligible and vested in the plan upon hire.'

terms.value_text: Immediate eligibility upon hire for all contribution types. Automatic enrollment at 3% pre-tax begins 35 days after hire date.
terms.source_snippet: You will become eligible to participate in the Plan for all contribution types immediately upon satisfying the above eligibility requirements.
terms.notes: Auto-enroll default deferral 3% of eligible compensation beginning 35 days after hire.

terms.value_text: No last-day-of-year employment requirement for regular employees to receive match/nonelective. (A last-day + 1,000-hour rule applies only to temporary/casual employees.)
terms.source_snippet: Temporary and casual employees will be eligible for matching contributions if they have earned at least 1,000 Hours of Service in the Plan year and are employed on the last day of the Plan year.
terms.notes: Regular employees have no last-day rule; requirement is limited to temporary/casual staff.

terms.value_text: Frozen / closed to new hires. The Aerospace Employees' Retirement Plan (AERP) is a defined-benefit pension. Benefit accruals were frozen effective October 1, 2018 (no new Variable Units accrue); AERP1 participants — generally those hired before Jan 1, 1993 and continuously employed who did not elect the Combined Retirement Program — continued accruing through Sept 30, 2023. Employees hired after April 1, 2005 were not covered by the DB plan; those hired 1993–2005 could elect in. New hires today receive retirement benefits through the 401(k) plan (match + nonelective), not the DB pension.
terms.source_snippet: Effective October 1, 2018, your benefit accruals under the Plan will be frozen. This means that you will no longer be eligible to accrue a benefit under the Plan...
terms.notes: Aerospace pays full cost of the AERP benefit for covered participants. Plan is PBGC-insured DB pension (AERP1 and AERP2 documents). Closed to new participants; frozen for most since 2018 and for AERP1 since Sept 30, 2023.

terms.value_text: Company HSA contribution for Anthem Premium CDHP enrollees only: $750 individual coverage / $1,500 family (employee plus at least one dependent). Prorated by hire/enrollment date. Anthem Basic CDHP is NOT eligible for company contributions.
terms.source_snippet: Aerospace will contribute to your HSA when you enroll in the Anthem Premium CDHP... $750 for Individual ... $1,500 for Family (employee plus at least one dependent)
terms.notes: 2025 IRS HSA maximums referenced: $4,300 individual / $8,550 family. Employer seed deposited at beginning of year, prorated for mid-year hires (e.g., $375 for 6 months of individual coverage).

terms.value_text: Vacation: 15 paid days per year; 20 days per year after five years of service. (Separate sick time: exempt employees unlimited; nonexempt accrue 80 hours/year.)
terms.source_snippet: Vacation – 15 paid days per year; 20 days per year after five years of service
terms.notes: Vacation only; sick leave is separate and not counted here.

terms.value_text: Nine paid holidays.
terms.source_snippet: Holidays – Nine paid holidays

terms.value_text: Four weeks (160 hours) of company-paid parental leave, taken within the first 12 months of birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child.
terms.source_snippet: Parental Leave – Four weeks of company-paid leave when taken within the first 12 months of the birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child
terms.notes: Employees portal states maximum 160 hours of paid parental leave within 12 months.

terms.value_text: Educational Assistance includes tuition reimbursement, a fellowship program, educational assignment, and eligibility for Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF). Annual cap reported by third-party aggregator at approximately $15,000; not confirmed on an employer-published source.
terms.source_snippet: Educational Assistance – Tuition reimbursement, fellowship program, educational assignment, and eligibility for Public Service Loan Forgiveness (PSLF)
terms.notes: Program confirmed on employer total-rewards PDF; the ~$15,000/yr cap figure comes from third-party search results and is not verified against an employer/plan document.

terms.value_text: Full-time employees may work either a standard eight-hour day or a 9/80 compressed schedule with alternate Fridays off.
terms.source_snippet: Work Hours – Full-time employees can work either a standard eight-hour day or a 9/80 schedule with alternate Fridays off
terms.notes: Telework flexibility also offered based on role and business needs.

terms.value_text: Short-term disability income replacement for up to 52 weeks (California SDI: 60% of weekly salary up to $1,620/week for up to 52 weeks; non-California STD via third-party insurer, up to 52 weeks). Long-term disability begins after 180 days.
terms.source_snippet: California State Disability Insurance (SDI): The weekly benefit is 60 percent of weekly salary up to a maximum of $1,620 per week for up to 52 weeks.
terms.notes: STD supplemented by company sick leave. Group LTD provides monthly income after 180 days of total disability.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic employee life insurance of one times annual salary, maximum $200,000. Optional supplemental employee-paid life and accident coverage available.
terms.source_snippet: Basic employee life insurance coverage of one times annual salary, maximum $200,000
terms.notes: Company-paid basic plus occupational accident coverage per Benefits Highlights.

terms.value_text: Amentum 401(k) Retirement Plan: $0.50 of every dollar up to the first 6% of eligible pay (50% of first 6%), maximum match 3%. (Note: Legacy Jacobs employees on the separate Jacobs 401(k) plan received $0.75 per $1 up to 6%, up to 4.5% max.)
terms.source_snippet: the Amentum 401(k) Retirement Plan with a maximum matching contribution of $.50 of every dollar contributed up to the first 6% of eligible pay (maximum matching contribution of 3%)
terms.notes: From FY2025 DEF 14A (proxy) describing FY2024 NEO benefits. Legacy Amentum and Legacy Jacobs still ran separate 401(k) plans in FY2024 post-merger; match varies by legacy plan / CBA / SCA contract.

terms.value_text: Employer matching contributions 100% vested at all times (immediate); participant, rollover and Roth contributions immediately vested. May differ where a CBA/contract specifies a vesting schedule.
terms.source_snippet: Vesting of discretionary matching contributions is 100% at all times, unless specified otherwise in the contract or collective bargaining agreement.
terms.notes: Third-party plan-doc aggregator; consistent with DEF 14A reference to 'accrued, vested balances under the defined contribution retirement plan, including vested employer contributions.' Marked inferred as the explicit vesting schedule was not confirmed in an employer/SEC document.

terms.value_text: 401(k) plan available with year-round enrollment (including part-time/variable/temporary employees); automatic enrollment with elective deferrals via payroll.
terms.source_snippet: the Employee Assistance Program (EAP), business travel benefits and the 401(k) plan with year-round enrollment
terms.notes: Immediate/year-round eligibility from the official Core Benefits Guide; auto-enrollment detail corroborated by Built In.

terms.value_text: No defined-benefit pension. Retirement is defined-contribution (401(k)) only. Neither Legacy Amentum nor Legacy Jacobs maintained a DB pension plan covering NEOs in FY2024.
terms.source_snippet: Neither Legacy Amentum nor Jacobs maintained a defined benefit pension plan that covered the NEOs during fiscal year 2024.
terms.notes: Legacy site-specific/CBA pension obligations may exist at some government contracts, but no company-wide DB pension for employees.

terms.value_text: Up to $100 per quarter (up to $400/year) contributed to the HSA on completing quarterly wellness activities. Requires enrollment in Anthem HDHP or HDHP Plus. PPO plans are not HSA-eligible.
terms.source_snippet: Amentum will contribute up to $100 per quarter if you complete wellness activities ... The HSA contribution limit has increased to $3,650 per year for an individual and $7,300 per year for a family.
terms.notes: Wellness-contingent; not a flat contribution. Not tiered by coverage level - flat $100/qtr per eligible employee. From 2022 One Amentum Core Benefits Guide.

terms.value_text: Tiered PTO accrual of roughly 10-25 days depending on years of service; varies significantly by contract, SCA wage determination and CBA.
terms.source_snippet: tiered PTO accrual by tenure ... 9-10 holidays/15-25 PTO day structures across sites
terms.notes: Third-party summary; entry level ~10 days used as value_num. Highly contract/location dependent.

terms.value_text: Approximately 10 paid holidays per year, plus 2 floating holidays (8 hrs each) after one year of service. Varies by contract/SCA/CBA (9-10 typical).
terms.source_snippet: ten (10) paid holidays per year ... two floating holidays (8 hours each) after completion of one full year of service
terms.notes: Third-party summary; varies by contract/site.

terms.value_text: Tax-free education assistance up to $5,250/year for regular full-time employees ($2,625 for regular part-time) per calendar year, with a final passing grade of C or higher.
terms.source_snippet: tax-free tuition reimbursement ... up to $5,250 if you are a regular full-time employee or $2,625 if you are a regular part-time employee, each calendar year
terms.notes: From official Core Benefits Guide; matches IRS Section 127 max. Continuing-service agreements may apply per employee reports.

terms.value_text: Company-paid short-term disability at 66.67% of weekly base earnings, up to $2,000/week, beginning after 7 consecutive days of absence, payable up to 26 weeks. Administered by The Hartford.
terms.source_snippet: company-paid STD coverage at 66.67% of their weekly base earnings, up to $2,000 per week ... payable up to a maximum of 26 weeks from date of disability
terms.notes: LTD follows at 60% of weekly base (up to $15,000/mo) after 180 days.

terms.value_text: Company-provided basic life and basic AD&D coverage at 1x base annual salary. Reduces 35% at age 70, 50% at age 75. Optional/supplemental up to 8x salary available for purchase.
terms.source_snippet: Most employees will receive company-provided basic life and basic AD&D coverage at one times their base annual salary.
terms.notes: Administered by The Hartford. From 2022 Core Benefits Guide.

terms.value_text: Amentum Holdings Employee Stock Purchase Plan (Section 423 qualified): purchase common stock at a 5% discount to fair market value on the last day of the offering period. Adopted 2024-09-27, effective 2025-07-01.
terms.source_snippet: right to purchase shares of common stock at a 5% discount of the market value on the last day of the offering period
terms.notes: ESPP established after the Sept 2024 IPO/merger; corroborated by AMTM FY2025 10-K. 5% discount, no lookback.

terms.value_text: Anduril offers a 401(k) plan but does not provide an employer matching contribution. The company frames equity (RSUs/options) and ESPP access as its ownership-based alternative to a guaranteed match.
terms.source_snippet: No 401(k) match ... Anduril compensates through equity, with RSUs, options for more tenured employees, and ESPP access.
terms.notes: Third-party only (private company; no SEC filing). Multiple sources (Glassdoor reviews, freyrgroup, Built In FAQ) consistently state no employer 401(k) match. Marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Flexible / uncapped paid time off for salaried employees (no fixed day allotment); advance notice to manager required. A bonus is reportedly paid when taking a vacation longer than one week.
terms.source_snippet: Anduril follows a flexible time off policy for salaried employees, meaning there is no cap on the number of days you can take off, but you must provide your manager with advance notice.
terms.notes: Flexible/unlimited PTO, so no numeric minimum. Third-party summary of the 2025 benefits overview; marked inferred.

terms.value_text: 16 weeks of 100% paid parental leave for all eligible employees. Additional new-parent resources: doula support, SNOO bassinet rentals, and lactation specialists.
terms.source_snippet: Maternity: 16 weeks; Paternity: 16 weeks. All employees are eligible for 16 weeks of 100% paid parental leave.
terms.notes: Consistent across Levels.fyi and the 2025 benefits overview summary. Third-party only; marked inferred.

terms.value_text: HSA available when enrolled in the High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP). Anduril covers 100% of the employee premium on the HDHP (and Kaiser HMO), removing the coverage cost, but no specific employer HSA seed/contribution dollar amount was found in public sources.
terms.source_snippet: Anduril covers 100% of the premiums on its High-Deductible Health Plan, which means employees can enroll in an HSA without paying anything for the underlying coverage.
terms.notes: No employer HSA contribution/seed dollar figure disclosed publicly. Only the 100% HDHP premium coverage is documented. value_num left null.

terms.value_text: Anduril offers company equity (RSUs, and options for more tenured employees) plus an Employee Stock Purchase Plan (ESPP). No published ESPP discount percentage found. As a private/pre-IPO company, ESPP-purchased and vested shares are not yet liquid; offers commonly use a 4-year equity vesting schedule.
terms.source_snippet: Employees have access to an Employee Stock Purchase Plan ... RSUs and options ... four-year vesting common in offers ... shares are not yet liquid.
terms.notes: Equity captured per task instruction. No ESPP discount % disclosed; value_num null. Private company, shares illiquid pre-IPO. Third-party only; marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Immediate eligibility - an Eligible Employee may begin contributions on any Enrollment Date. Employees hired/rehired on or after January 1, 2017 are automatically enrolled at an initial 3% before-tax rate, auto-escalating 1%/year up to 6%.
terms.source_snippet: if you are hired or rehired ... on or after January 1, 2017 ... you will be automatically enrolled in the Plan to make Before-Tax Contributions at an initial rate of 3% of Compensation
terms.notes: From the official BAE Systems Employees' Savings and Investment Plan Summary Plan Description. Auto-enrollment does not apply to collectively bargained employees or the Intelligence & Security (I&S) Sector.

terms.value_text: BAE Systems makes an automatic non-elective ('free') company contribution in addition to any match. Third-party summary cites a 5% free base contribution for typical units; the SPD confirms Nonelective Contributions are made 'on a non-discretionary basis' where specified in the Plan Supplement. Exact percentage varies by business unit.
terms.source_snippet: A Nonelective Contribution means an Employer Contribution made on a non-discretionary basis to the Plan on behalf of its Employees, as may be described in your Plan Supplement.
terms.notes: SPD (sec/employer) confirms the non-elective employer contribution structure; the specific 5% headline is from a third-party benefits summary and varies by BAE business unit / Plan Supplement.

terms.value_text: Employer matching contribution. Levels.fyi reports 100% match on the first 4% of base salary. Match formula varies by BAE business unit: some units historically 75% on the first 10% (up to 7.5% of pay); effective Jan 1, 2025 the Shared Services IT match increased from 75% to 100% on the first 6%.
terms.source_snippet: 401(k): 100% match on the first 4% of base salary
terms.notes: Match formula is set per Plan Supplement and differs across BAE business units; treat value_num as one representative unit. SPD confirms Matching Contributions exist.

terms.value_text: Employees are always 100% vested in their own contributions. Employer contributions are subject to a vesting schedule described in each Plan Supplement (immediate, cliff, or graded, varying by unit); employees widely report the company match/contribution is fully (immediately) vested.
terms.source_snippet: The plan matches up to 6 percent, and the 6% match is vested the day you start.
terms.notes: SPD: 'you will be vested in any Employer Contributions based on the vesting schedule described in your Plan Supplement.' Vesting varies by business unit; value_num 0 reflects the commonly reported immediate vesting.

terms.value_text: Defined-benefit pension closed to new hires. BAE Systems offered a DB retirement income plan to employees hired before January 1, 2010; employees hired on/after that date are not eligible and instead participate in the 401(k) Savings Plan.
terms.source_snippet: BAE Systems offers a defined benefit retirement income plan to employees hired before January 1, 2010.
terms.notes: DB plan closed to new hires (pre-2010 hires only). Newer hires rely on the 401(k) with automatic company contribution plus match.

terms.value_text: BAE Systems contributes to the employee HSA when enrolled in an HSA-eligible medical plan, even if the employee makes no contribution. Specific dollar amount not publicly disclosed (behind benefits login).
terms.source_snippet: BAE Systems will contribute to your HSA if you are eligible to contribute to an HSA, even if you do not.
terms.notes: Employer HSA seed confirmed; tier/coverage dollar matrix not available on public pages.

terms.value_text: Entry paid time off roughly 3 weeks (~15 days), with about 6 months' accrual granted up front at hire; accrual increases after 5 years of service.
terms.source_snippet: starting PTO is 3 weeks off when you start out with 6 months worth given up front
terms.notes: Employee-reported (third-party); exact accrual schedule varies by tenure and business unit.

terms.value_text: Paid parental leave approximately 4 weeks (Levels.fyi lists 4 weeks maternity and 4 weeks paternity); can be used in addition to other leave programs, with adoption and surrogacy reimbursement available.
terms.source_snippet: Maternity Leave: 4 weeks; Paternity Leave: 4 weeks
terms.notes: Third-party figure; program described as generous paid parental leave on employer/Built In pages.

terms.value_text: Tuition reimbursement program offered for degree programs and advanced education; annual dollar cap not publicly disclosed (behind benefits login).
terms.source_snippet: BAE Systems offers a tuition reimbursement program, so you can pursue advanced education to help further your career.
terms.notes: Existence confirmed; annual maximum not available on public pages.

terms.value_text: 9/80 compressed work schedule offered at many sites, giving every other Friday off (role/site dependent).
terms.source_snippet: 9/80 schedule, which allows for every other Friday off
terms.notes: Third-party/employer marketing; availability depends on role, program, and site.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic life insurance equal to $50,000 or two times annual base salary, whichever is greater, up to a maximum of $500,000.
terms.source_snippet: Life Insurance: Equaling $50,000 or two times the annual base salary, whichever is greater (up to a maximum of $500,000)
terms.notes: Third-party figure. LTD separately reported at 60% of salary up to a monthly maximum.

terms.value_text: Employer matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of pay (i.e. 3% max match) via the Battelle Employees' Savings Plan, administered by Vanguard. Before-tax, after-tax and Roth contributions allowed.
terms.source_snippet: The 401k is 50% match to 6% plus a corporate 5% contribution.
terms.notes: Third-party (Glassdoor) lead; not confirmed against an employer PDF/SPD. Plan formally = Battelle Employees' Savings Plan (Vanguard, plan #3859).

terms.value_text: Additional non-elective 'corporate' employer contribution of 5% into the Battelle Employees' Savings Plan, on top of the 50%-up-to-6% match.
terms.source_snippet: The 401k is 50% match to 6% plus a corporate 5% contribution.
terms.notes: Third-party (Glassdoor) lead; the 5% corporate/non-elective contribution appears to be the primary retirement vehicle after the DB pension was closed to new hires.

terms.value_text: Closed to new hires and frozen. Only employees hired prior to July 1, 2015 have a defined-benefit pension benefit. Multiple Battelle DB plans confirmed: The 2017 Employees' Pension Plan of Battelle Memorial Institute (frozen - no new benefit accruals), the Employees Pension Plan of Battelle Memorial Institute (DB), and the Pension Plan of the Pacific Northwest Laboratories Battelle Memorial Institute (PNNL, DB).
terms.source_snippet: This is a 'frozen' plan, meaning that as of the last day of the plan year, the plan provides that no participant will get any new benefit accrual
terms.notes: CONFIRMS Form 5500 lead: multiple Battelle DB pensions exist. 'Prior to July 1, 2015' eligibility cutoff is from Battelle's own Benefit Options at Termination Guide (employer PDF, retrieved via search summary; direct fetch blocked with HTTP 403). Frozen status verified via Form 5500/QDRO record for the 2017 plan. Pension Service Center: myplansconnect.com/battelle.

terms.value_text: Battelle makes a one-time employer contribution of $500 to the employee HSA (HealthEquity administered). Not an annual recurring seed per third-party reports.
terms.source_snippet: Battelle makes a one-time contribution of $500 to employees' HSA accounts.
terms.notes: Third-party lead; HSA administered by HealthEquity per Battelle termination guide. Amount/tier not confirmed against employer SPD.

terms.value_text: PTO/vacation reported at roughly 20-30 days per year depending on tenure; using entry/minimum ~20 days.
terms.source_snippet: Battelle Memorial Institute's PTO and Vacation policy typically gives 20-30 days off a year.
terms.notes: Third-party (Comparably) range; entry-level likely toward the low end.

terms.value_text: Paid parental leave of about 1 month for both mothers and fathers; additional time available via PTO, Short-Term Disability, and FMLA.
terms.source_snippet: Parental leave of 1 month is offered for mom and dad. Additional time is available through a combination of PTO, Short-Term Disability and FMLA.
terms.notes: Third-party lead; ~1 month approximated as 4 weeks.

terms.value_text: 9/80 compressed schedule offered: employees work 80 hours over 9 days with every other Friday off.
terms.source_snippet: Many employees work a 9/80 schedule with every other Friday off. Employees work 80 hours in nine days instead of 10.
terms.notes: Third-party lead corroborated across multiple sources.

terms.value_text: Tuition/education assistance program offered, but the annual dollar cap is not publicly disclosed in employer or third-party sources reviewed.
terms.source_snippet: Battelle ... provides 'generous PTO', includes 'tuition assistance', and maintains health insurance with HSA eligibility.
terms.notes: Program existence confirmed; no citable dollar cap found. Direct employer page fetch returned HTTP 403; text obtained via WebFetch summary.

terms.value_text: Non-union (salaried) Boeing 401(k) Retirement Plan (formerly VIP): dollar-for-dollar match on the first 10% of eligible base + incentive pay contributed (Pretax, Roth, Catch-Up). Raised to 100% of first 10% in 2022 when the age-based VIP+ company contribution was folded into the match. IAM union employees (2024 contract) get a separate formula: 100% match up to 8% of pay.
terms.source_snippet: Boeing makes a dollar-for-dollar match on the first 10% of pay contributed
terms.notes: Employer careers page. Union (IAM) match differs: 100% up to 8% per 2024 contract (plansponsor.com). Match formula for non-union confirmed by Kingsview and TrueWealth.

terms.value_text: No separate automatic/non-elective company contribution for non-union employees as of 2022. The former age-based VIP+ / SSP+ company contributions (3%-5% depending on age, and a 9%/8%/7% transition contribution in 2016/2017/2018 after the pension freeze) were discontinued beginning 2022 and consolidated into the enhanced 100%-of-first-10% match.
terms.source_snippet: in 2022, Boeing raised that match to 100% of all non-union employee contributions up to a cap of 10% of their salary
terms.notes: Historically Boeing paid an age-based automatic DC contribution after freezing the pension (9% 2016, 8% 2017, 7% 2018, then 3%-5% age-based); per theretirementgroup.com the age-based VIP+/SSP+ contributions ceased in 2022 and were rolled into the 10% match. Current separate auto contribution = 0.

terms.value_text: Company matching contributions are 100% vested immediately (employees are fully vested in company contributions at all times).
terms.source_snippet: Immediate vesting in the 401(k) plan means you gain ownership of Boeing's contributions right away.
terms.notes: Consistent across multiple advisor sources summarizing Boeing 401(k) Plan (VIP).

terms.value_text: Match eligibility begins the first of the month following hire date, for employees age 18 or older. Effectively immediate / near-immediate.
terms.source_snippet: Eligibility for the match starts from the first of the month following your hire date, provided you are 18 years or older.
terms.notes: Third-party advisor summary; no fixed multi-month waiting period.

terms.value_text: Frozen / closed to new hires. Boeing froze the salaried (nonunion) defined-benefit pension for ~68,000 employees effective January 1, 2016 (announced March 2014); accruals stopped and employees moved to defined-contribution 401(k) with a company contribution. Union (IAM) pension was frozen earlier (~2014-2016) and was NOT restored in the 2024 IAM contract. Benefits accrued before the freeze are still payable.
terms.source_snippet: Boeing will freeze pensions for 68,000 nonunion employees
terms.notes: Announced via boeing.mediaroom.com 2014-03-06; freeze effective 2016. plansponsor.com confirms the frozen pension was not restored in the 2024 IAM union deal. No DB pension for new hires.

terms.value_text: Approximately $2,000/year employer contribution to the HSA for employees enrolled in a qualifying high-deductible health plan (per Levels.fyi crowd data). Exact amount varies by plan option and coverage tier.
terms.source_snippet: $2,000 per year contributed by employer
terms.notes: Third-party aggregator figure; Boeing careers page confirms an employer HSA contribution exists but does not publish the dollar amount. Actual amount depends on plan/coverage tier.

terms.value_text: Non-union salaried employees choose between PTO (single bank) or separate Vacation + Sick banks. Total time off typically ranges ~20-30 days/year, increasing with tenure. Entry-level roughly 2-3 weeks vacation.
terms.source_snippet: Boeing offers two primary paid-time off programs for U.S. salaried, nonunion employees: Paid Time Off (PTO) and Vacation and Sick Leave.
terms.notes: Entry number approximate; Glassdoor/Comparably report 20-30 days total with accrual rising by tenure.

terms.value_text: Up to ~12 paid holidays per year including a paid year-end shutdown week (roughly Christmas through New Year) plus one floating holiday.
terms.source_snippet: Employees are given as much as 12 paid holidays in a year, and also get a 7-day paid vacation between Christmas and New Year holidays.
terms.notes: Includes Boeing's traditional year-end shutdown; count varies slightly by site/union.

terms.value_text: 12 weeks of full pay paid parental leave for a birth, adoption, surrogacy, or placement of a foster child. Also: adoption assistance up to $10,000 per adoption (max 2) and surrogacy assistance up to $15,000 lifetime.
terms.source_snippet: 12 weeks of full pay for a birth, adoption, surrogacy or placement of a foster child
terms.notes: Employer careers page.

terms.value_text: Learning Together Program tuition assistance: up to $15,000/year for bachelor's degrees and up to $25,000/year for graduate degrees.
terms.source_snippet: up to $15,000 per year for bachelor degrees ... up to $25,000 per year for graduate degrees
terms.notes: Headline value_num uses graduate cap; bachelor cap is $15,000/year. Employer careers page.

terms.value_text: Dollar-for-dollar up to 6% of eligible comp (ECAP 401k)
terms.source_snippet: The 401K program offers a dollar-for-dollar annual matching contribution of up to 6 percent of eligible compensation

terms.value_text: Match immediately vested
terms.source_snippet: The annual matching contribution is immediately vested

terms.value_text: First 60 days to elect/opt out; else auto-enrolled at 6% pre-tax
terms.source_snippet: During the first 60 days ... automatically enrolled in the ECAP 401K with a 6 percent pre-tax contribution

terms.value_text: Must be active on Dec 31 (last day of plan year) to receive the match
terms.source_snippet: you must be an active employee on the last day of the plan year (December 31) to be eligible for the matching contribution

terms.value_text: No DB pension; DC ECAP 401(k) only
terms.source_snippet: The Employees' Capital Accumulation Plan (ECAP) is a tax-deferred, defined contribution plan

terms.value_text: ~$250 single/$500 family (Platinum); $150/$300 (Gold) — third-party, NOT in BAH PDF
terms.source_snippet: Platinum Plan: $250 for an individual, $500 for a family. Gold Plan: $150 ... $300
terms.notes: BAH slick sheet names CDHP tiers Gold/Bronze with no dollar figures; verify via crowdsourcing.

terms.value_text: Accrual by level/tenure: 16→25 days (VP 30)
terms.source_snippet: ANNUAL ACCRUAL (DAYS) - Admin Professional, Level 1, Level 2: 16 / 18 / 20 / 25 ... Vice President: 30

terms.value_text: Up to 6 wks paid parental leave (birth/adoption), plus up to 24 wks unpaid
terms.source_snippet: Mothers and fathers receive up to 6 weeks of paid leave after the birth or adoption of a child

terms.value_text: FlexEd, tenure-tiered: $5,250 / $7,500 / $10,000
terms.source_snippet: 0-LESS THAN 2 YEARS: $5,250 | 2 TO LESS THAN 5 YEARS: $7,500 | 5+ YEARS: $10,000

terms.value_text: Not published; only generic 'flexible work arrangements'
terms.source_snippet: endorsing flexible work arrangements

terms.value_text: ESPP: 5% discount on Booz Allen stock
terms.source_snippet: purchase Booz Allen stock at a 5 percent discount

terms.value_text: 11 fixed paid holidays/yr
terms.source_snippet: The firm offers 11 fixed paid holidays each year

terms.value_text: 50% match on the first 8% of pay contributed (i.e., $0.50 per dollar up to 8% of pay), yielding an effective employer match of ~4% of salary. Plan: CACI $MART 401(k) Retirement Plan.
terms.source_snippet: For every dollar an employee contributes, up to 8% of their pay, CACI will add 50 cents.
terms.notes: value_num reflects the 8% of pay eligible for match; effective employer contribution is ~4% of salary. Consistent with SEC 11-K descriptions of Company matching contributions.

terms.value_text: Salary deferrals vest immediately. Company matching and discretionary profit-sharing contributions become 100% vested after three years of continuous service (cliff).
terms.source_snippet: Participants become 100% vested in Company matching and profit-sharing contributions after three years of continuous service.
terms.notes: From CACI $MART Plan Form 11-K. Also referenced in CACI FY2025 10-K: minimum matching contributions vest after three years of continuous service.

terms.value_text: No traditional defined-benefit pension for U.S. employees. CACI sponsors defined-contribution plans only (CACI $MART Plan, a 401(k)), plus a non-qualified Group Executive/Supplemental Retirement Savings Plan for certain key employees.
terms.source_snippet: The Company sponsors defined contribution plans... The CACI $MART Plan... The Company makes minimum matching contributions that vest after three years of continuous service.
terms.notes: Defined-contribution only; no DB pension identified for general employees.

terms.value_text: Employee Stock Purchase Plan: employees may purchase CACI stock at 95% of market price (5% discount; CACI covers the remaining 5%).
terms.source_snippet: Employees may purchase CACI stock at 95% of the market price, with CACI covering the remaining 5%.
terms.notes: 5% discount ESPP.

terms.value_text: Employer contributes approximately $750/year to the HSA for employees enrolled in a qualifying high-deductible health plan.
terms.source_snippet: Health Savings Account (HSA) with $750 per year contributed by employer.
terms.notes: Third-party aggregator figure; coverage tier (self vs family) not specified in source. Not confirmed against an employer PDF.

terms.value_text: Tuition reimbursement up to $12,000/year for full-time employees ($6,000 for part-time) per fiscal year (July 1 - June 30).
terms.source_snippet: Full-time employees may receive up to $12,000 and part-time employees may receive up to $6,000 in tuition reimbursement per fiscal year (July 1 - June 30).
terms.notes: Third-party figure; not confirmed against an employer benefits PDF.

terms.value_text: Paid Parental Leave on birth or adoption of a child under 18: full-time employees eligible for 4 weeks, part-time for 2 weeks.
terms.source_snippet: Paid Parental Leave... full-time employees eligible for 4 weeks and part-time employees eligible for 2 weeks.
terms.notes: Sourced from CACI Leave Programs page description via search summary; page returned 404/403 on direct fetch, so marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Six fixed company holidays on which CACI offices are closed.
terms.source_snippet: CACI provides six fixed holidays for which CACI offices are closed.
terms.notes: Sourced from CACI Leave Programs page via search summary; marked inferred pending PDF confirmation.

terms.value_text: Flexible Time Off (FTO) for full-time salaried employees: not banked/accrued, no fixed day count, approval-based per business need. Hourly employees receive a semi-monthly accrual starting day one, increasing with length of service. No payout on exit for FTO.
terms.source_snippet: Flexible Time Off (FTO)... allows people to take a break... in accordance with business need and with prior approval from their manager.
terms.notes: No fixed entry-day minimum for salaried employees due to FTO model; third-party aggregators estimate ~20 days. value_num left null because no guaranteed banked amount.

terms.value_text: Safe harbor matching contribution of 100% of the first 4% of salary deferrals (dollar-for-dollar to 4%).
terms.source_snippet: The Plan also provides a safe harbor matching contribution of 100% of the first 4% of salary deferrals as described by Section 401(K)(12) of the Internal Revenue Code.
terms.notes: From the FY2020 Form 11-K (Cubic Corporation 401(k) Retirement Plan), the most recent SEC filing before Cubic was taken private by Veritas Capital (May 2021); no newer filings exist. NOTE: the Company temporarily froze matching contributions effective Apr 1, 2020 due to COVID-19 and reinstated them effective Oct 1, 2020. Earlier FY2019 11-K described a dollar-for-dollar match on the first 3% (4% for Cubic Worldwide Technical Services employees); the plan was subsequently converted to the 4% safe harbor design reported here.

terms.value_text: Discretionary (profit-sharing) Company contribution at the option of the Board of Directors, allocated by ratio of participant compensation to total eligible compensation. Not a fixed percentage; none was made for FY2020.
terms.source_snippet: The Plan provides for a Company discretionary contribution, at the option of its Board of Directors... Also, no discretionary contribution was made to the Plan for the Plan year ended September 30, 2020.
terms.notes: This is a discretionary employer contribution, separate from the 4% safe harbor match. Amount varies year to year and can be zero.

terms.value_text: Employer matching (safe harbor) and rollover contributions are immediately 100% vested. Employer discretionary contributions vest 20% after 1 year of service, increasing 20% per year to full vesting after 5 years of service.
terms.source_snippet: Employee, Company matching, Sub Plan discretionary and rollover contributions... have full and immediate vesting. Employer discretionary contributions... vest after one year of service at 20% and increase in 20% increments until fully vested after five years of service.
terms.notes: value_num=5 reflects the graded vesting for discretionary employer contributions; the safe harbor match is immediately (0-year) vested. Accounts also become fully vested upon death, disability, normal retirement age, layoff, or plan termination.

terms.value_text: Last-day rule applies to the discretionary employer contribution only: participants must be employed as of the Plan's year end to be eligible. The safe harbor match has no last-day requirement.
terms.source_snippet: Eligible Plan participants must be employed by the Company as of the Plan's year end to be eligible for a discretionary contribution.
terms.notes: Applies only to the discretionary/profit-sharing contribution, not to the 4% safe harbor match (safe harbor matches cannot carry a last-day rule).

terms.value_text: Frozen and closed to new hires. The U.S. noncontributory defined benefit pension plan was frozen (curtailed) as of December 31, 2006; no new benefits accrue and employees hired after that date are not eligible. (A separate European plan was frozen Sep 30, 2010.)
terms.source_snippet: Certain employees in the U.S. are covered by a noncontributory defined benefit pension plan for which benefits were frozen as of December 31, 2006 (curtailment)... U.S. and European employees hired subsequent to the dates of the curtailment of the respective plans are not eligible for participation in the defined benefit plans.
terms.notes: From FY2020 Form 10-K. U.S. DB pension is a legacy frozen plan; effectively new/current employees rely on the 401(k). No indication of reopening after the 2021 take-private.

terms.value_text: Health Savings Account (HSA) offered alongside a high-deductible plan option; FSA also offered. Employer HSA contribution amount not disclosed in public sources.
terms.source_snippet: Offers Health Savings Account (HSA). Offers Flexible Spending Account (FSA).
terms.notes: Third-party (Built In) confirms HSA is offered; no employer seed/contribution figure published. Marked inferred (third-party only).

terms.value_text: Paid time off / vacation reported at roughly 15-20 days per year by aggregators (Indeed respondents cite 10-20 days), increasing with tenure. Entry level not precisely published.
terms.source_snippet: Cubic's PTO and Vacation policy typically gives 15-20 days a year; Indeed respondents said 10-20 days.
terms.notes: Third-party estimate only; no employer-published day count. value_num=15 is a mid/entry estimate. Marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Approximately 6 weeks paid maternity leave per employee reports; policy covers birth, adoption, surrogacy and foster care. Payment level (full vs partial) reported inconsistently.
terms.source_snippet: 6 weeks maternity leave offered (some reviews note 6 weeks half paid).
terms.notes: Glassdoor employee reports only; no employer-published parental leave duration. Marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Tuition reimbursement / education assistance program offered; annual cap not publicly disclosed.
terms.source_snippet: Cubic supports furthering your education with a tuition reimbursement program (per aggregator listings).
terms.notes: Existence of the program is corroborated by multiple aggregators, but no dollar cap is published; some sources conflict on whether it is offered. Marked inferred.

terms.value_text: 9/80 compressed schedule offered at many sites: nine-hour days with every other Friday off.
terms.source_snippet: The 9/80 schedule (nine-hour days with every other Friday off) creates predictable time off.
terms.notes: Employee reviews (Indeed) reference a 9/80 schedule; not confirmed by an employer-published document. Marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Employee Stock Purchase Plan offered a 5% discount off market price on the last trading day of the offering period; annual contributions capped at $25,000.
terms.source_snippet: of 5% of the market price per share on the last trading day of the offering period. Annual employee contributions are limited to $25,000, are voluntary, and made through a bi-weekly payroll deduction.
terms.notes: HISTORICAL / NO LONGER AVAILABLE: this ESPP required publicly traded Cubic stock. Cubic was taken private by Veritas Capital in May 2021 and delisted, so the ESPP is defunct. Included for completeness from the last (FY2020) 10-K.

terms.value_text: Draper contributes an amount equal to 10% of base salary to the retirement plan, provided the employee contributes a minimum of 5% on a tax-deferred basis. Effectively a $2-for-$1 arrangement on the first 5% of salary. Plan is a 403(b) (nonprofit), administered by TIAA; ROTH and pre-tax options offered.
terms.source_snippet: Employees contribute a minimum of 5% of their base salary on a tax-deferred basis, and Draper contributes an amount equal to 10%
terms.notes: Nonprofit uses a 403(b), not a 401(k). Employer 10% is contingent on the mandatory 5% employee deferral, so it functions as both a match and a non-elective contribution. Glassdoor corroborates '$2 for $1 on the first 5%'.

terms.value_text: New participants begin at 50% vested in employer contributions and earn an additional 10% per year, reaching 100% vesting after 5 years of participation.
terms.source_snippet: New participants begin with 50% vesting in employer contributions, earning an additional 10% annually.
terms.notes: Graded vesting: 50% at entry, +10%/yr to 100% at 5 years.

terms.value_text: FROZEN. Draper maintains a defined-benefit pension (Retirement Plan for Employees of The Charles Stark Draper Laboratory, Inc.), structured as a cash balance plan. The plan is frozen: 'as of the last day of the plan year, the plan provides that no participant will get any new benefit accrual.' The subsidy and the minimum pension benefit were frozen as of December 31, 2008, so base salary and years of service after that date are disregarded. Plan still exists and files Form 5500, but no new accruals occur.
terms.source_snippet: This is a 'frozen' plan, meaning that as of the last day of the plan year, the plan provides that no participant will get any new benefit accrual
terms.notes: Confirms Form 5500 lead: the DB pension still exists (hence Form 5500 filings) but is FROZEN. Freeze effective Dec 31, 2008 per QDRO plan-summary sources; cash balance formula. Current active-employee retirement benefit is the 403(b) 10% contribution described above. Freeze date is from a third-party QDRO summary of the SPD (SPD PDF itself timed out on fetch); status 'frozen' is well corroborated.

terms.value_text: Tuition assistance for employees working 20+ hours/week: up to $5,250/year for undergraduate programs, up to $20,000/year for graduate programs, and up to $3,000/year for job-related certificates.
terms.source_snippet: up to $5,250 annually for undergraduate programs and up to $20,000 annually for graduate programs ... up to $3,000 per year for job-related certificates
terms.notes: value_num uses the graduate cap ($20,000, the headline max). Undergrad cap $5,250, certificates $3,000.

terms.value_text: Paid parental leave following birth or adoption. Employer careers page confirms paid parental leave is offered but does not state weeks. Third-party sources report ~8 weeks paid maternity (recent) with an older source citing 12 weeks; paternity paid for a few days.
terms.source_snippet: Maternity is paid for 8 weeks, even when not eligible for government leaves.
terms.notes: Existence of paid parental leave is reported by employer; duration (8 wks) is third-party/inferred and conflicts with an older 12-week figure.

terms.value_text: Approximately 11 paid holidays per year.
terms.source_snippet: 26 days off in addition to 11 paid holidays
terms.notes: Employer careers page confirms 'paid holidays' but no count; 11 is from Glassdoor employee reports (inferred).

terms.value_text: Paid vacation plus generous sick leave. Employer page lists 'paid vacation time' and 'generous sick leave' without a day count. Employee reports are mixed: some cite a ~20-day vacation norm (with pushback above 20), others describe effectively unlimited vacation/sick time.
terms.source_snippet: incredible vacation days ... can be pretty stingy about PTO if you exceed 20 days
terms.notes: Conflicting employee reports (roughly 20 days vs. 'unlimited'); employer does not publish a number. value_num=20 as the commonly cited norm; inferred.

terms.value_text: Compressed 9/80 schedule offered: work 80 hours over 9 days and take every other Friday off (~26 extra days off per year). Employer describes flexible/hybrid/remote work options.
terms.source_snippet: 9/80 work schedule where employees get every other Friday off, providing an additional 26 days off
terms.notes: Built In confirms 'flexible work schedule'; the specific 9/80 / every-other-Friday-off detail is from Glassdoor employee reports (inferred).

terms.value_text: Draper offers a Health Savings Account (HSA) with an employer contribution. Built In confirms 'Offers Health Savings Account (HSA)' and employees describe 'very high HSA contributions,' but no exact employer dollar amount is published.
terms.source_snippet: Offers Health Savings Account (HSA)
terms.notes: HSA with employer funding confirmed to exist; exact contribution amount and coverage-tier matrix not disclosed in public sources. value_num left null.

terms.value_text: Employer matching contribution offered under the Elbit Systems of America Consolidated 401(k) Savings Plan; match begins at enrollment. Employer contributions are discretionary. No exact match percentage disclosed in any public source.
terms.source_snippet: Elbit Systems of America offers a matching contribution to the 401k, with the match starting at enrollment.
terms.notes: Third-party (Glassdoor/QDRO/aggregator) only; no employer total-rewards PDF or SEC 11-K available (ESA is a private U.S. subsidiary of Elbit Systems Ltd.). Exact match formula not publicly disclosed.

terms.value_text: Non-elective (profit-sharing) employer contribution offered in addition to the match. Reportedly begins after the first year of service and increases at 5 years of service. Employer contributions are variable/discretionary based on company profits.
terms.source_snippet: non-matching contribution starting after the first year of service and increasing at 5 years of service; a 'Profit-Sharing Plan', where employer contributions are variable
terms.notes: Third-party only; exact percentage not disclosed.

terms.value_text: Approximately 5-year graded ('linear') vesting of employer contributions per third-party reports.
terms.source_snippet: The 401k has a 5 year linear vesting period.
terms.notes: Third-party/aggregator only; not confirmed against a plan SPD or Form 5500.

terms.value_text: No defined-benefit pension identified. Retirement benefit is a defined-contribution 401(k)/profit-sharing plan (Elbit Systems of America Consolidated 401(k) Savings Plan).
terms.source_snippet: a cash or deferred arrangement described in Code section 401(k) that is part of a qualified defined contribution plan
terms.notes: No DB pension mentioned in any employer or third-party source; inferred as none/not offered.

terms.value_text: Health Savings Account (HSA) offered alongside medical/dental/vision and FSA. No employer HSA seed/contribution amount disclosed publicly.
terms.source_snippet: health insurance, dental insurance, vision insurance, disability insurance, health savings accounts (HSA), and flexible spending accounts (FSA)
terms.notes: HSA availability noted by third-party; employer contribution dollar amount not found.

terms.value_text: Flexible Vacation & PTO. Employee-reported vacation of roughly 10-20 days; 10 used as minimum/entry figure.
terms.source_snippet: respondents said they received 10-20 days of vacation
terms.notes: Employee-survey estimate; no employer-published PTO accrual schedule found.

terms.value_text: Paid Parental Leave offered (maternity & paternity). Number of weeks not disclosed publicly.
terms.source_snippet: Competitive compensation & 401k program... Paid Parental Leave, and Generous Employee Referral Program
terms.notes: Careers page confirms availability; duration not stated.

terms.value_text: Tuition assistance / reimbursement offered; employees report using it toward degrees including an MBA. Annual cap not disclosed publicly.
terms.source_snippet: employees develop professionally through tuition assistance and flexible work schedules
terms.notes: Dollar cap not published.

terms.value_text: 9/80 compressed schedule offered at most locations, giving every other Friday off.
terms.source_snippet: Most locations offer a 9/80 schedule providing every other Friday off.
terms.notes: Reported via careers page / employee reviews; location-dependent.

terms.value_text: Company-paid Basic Life Insurance provided, plus voluntary Additional Life & AD&D. Salary multiple not disclosed.
terms.source_snippet: Basic Life Insurance and Additional Life & AD&D Insurances are available.
terms.notes: Basic life coverage multiple not published.

terms.value_text: 100% of the first 3% of eligible compensation plus 50% of the next 3% = 4.5% of pay at a 6% employee deferral. Paid on a bi-weekly basis. GDIT confirms a match when employees contribute 1%-6% of eligible compensation.
terms.source_snippet: matching contribution of 100% of the first 3% of eligible compensation contributed to the Plan, and 50% of the next 3% of eligible compensation contributed to the Plan. The Plan is intended to be a safe-harbor plan
terms.notes: This is the parent General Dynamics Corporation 401(k) QACA (safe-harbor) Plan formula, not a GDIT-only document, but it matches the GDIT-reported match range (employees contribute 1%-6%) in the 2023 GDIT New Hire Benefits PDF and Glassdoor's GDIT 4.5% figure. GD auto-escalation formula for auto-enrolled participants becomes 100% of first 4% + 50% of next 2% = 5%.

terms.value_text: Auto-enrolled at 6% of eligible compensation after 10 days of employment; can change election at any time. Eligible to participate upon hire.
terms.source_snippet: Employees are auto-enrolled with a contribution of 6% of their eligible compensation after 10 days of employment and can change the election at any time
terms.notes: GDIT-specific (2023 GDIT New Hire Benefits at a Glance). SEC 11-K states employees are eligible upon hire; auto-enrollment default deferral rate is 6%.

terms.value_text: Employees hired on or after Jan 1, 2020 are 100% vested in safe-harbor employer matching contributions after two years of continuous service. Employees hired before Jan 1, 2020 are immediately 100% vested in safe-harbor match. Non-safe-harbor matching and any employer discretionary contributions follow a three-year cliff.
terms.source_snippet: Participants hired on or after January 1, 2020, are 100% vested in safe harbor employer matching contributions and earnings thereon after two years of continuous service. Non-safe harbor matching contributions and employer discretionary contributions ... three-year cliff
terms.notes: Parent GD QACA Plan (safe-harbor) terms. Glassdoor lists GDIT match as immediately vested; per spec the SEC filing is trusted over the aggregator, so 2 years for post-2020 hires. Vesting overall does not exceed three years across GD business units.

terms.value_text: No last-day-of-plan-year employment requirement for the match; the employer match is funded on a bi-weekly (per-paycheck) basis.
terms.source_snippet: GDIT offers a competitive match on a bi-weekly basis when employees contribute 1%-6% of their eligible compensation
terms.notes: Inferred: a per-paycheck safe-harbor match implies no last-day-of-year rule, but the plan document was not read verbatim on this point.

terms.value_text: No defined-benefit pension for GDIT new hires; retirement is provided through the safe-harbor 401(k) plan. General Dynamics froze/closed its salaried DB pension plans to new participants years ago.
terms.source_snippet: The Plan is intended to be a safe-harbor plan as defined in the IRC.
terms.notes: Inferred from the fact that GD offers a safe-harbor 401(k) as the retirement vehicle and no DB pension is referenced in GDIT benefits materials; GD salaried defined-benefit pensions were frozen to new hires. Parent-GD context, not a GDIT-specific pension statement.

terms.value_text: Employees regularly scheduled to work 40 hours per week receive 10 paid (80 hours) holidays per calendar year; 30-39 hour employees receive pro-rated holiday pay.
terms.source_snippet: Employees regularly scheduled to work 40 hours per week receive 10 paid (80 hours) holidays per calendar year
terms.notes: GDIT-specific (2023 GDIT New Hire Benefits at a Glance).

terms.value_text: Paid family leave of 4 weeks / 160 hours in a rolling 12-month period (pro-rated for those working less than 40 hours/week) for care of a spouse, child or parent with a serious health condition, or the birth, adoption or foster placement of a child.
terms.source_snippet: Paid family leave of 4 weeks/160 hours in a rolling 12-month period ... for the care of a spouse, child or parent with a serious health condition or the birth, adoption or foster placement of a child
terms.notes: GDIT-specific. This is a combined paid family leave (bonding + family care), not a separate larger parental-only benefit.

terms.value_text: Company-provided short-term disability begins after a 7 calendar-day elimination period and continues up to 25 consecutive weeks following an accident or illness. LTD then begins after 26 consecutive weeks (50% or 60% of pay options, employee-paid).
terms.source_snippet: Benefits for this company-provided coverage will begin after a seven calendar-day elimination period and continue up to 25 consecutive weeks following an accident or illness
terms.notes: GDIT-specific.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic life and AD&D insurance equal to annual base pay (rounded up to the next higher $1,000). Voluntary supplemental life/AD&D and company-provided Business Travel Accident insurance also available.
terms.source_snippet: Eligible employees receive company-paid life and accidental death and dismemberment (AD&D) insurance equal to their annual base pay (rounded to the next higher $1,000)
terms.notes: GDIT-specific. Basic company-paid coverage = 1x annual base pay.

terms.value_text: Full flex workweeks allow employees to flex their time on a weekly or bi-weekly basis with management approval.
terms.source_snippet: Full flex workweeks allow employees to flex their time on a weekly or bi-weekly basis with management approval
terms.notes: Inferred: GDIT offers 'full flex workweeks' (weekly/bi-weekly flexing) with manager approval rather than an explicitly named 9/80 schedule; availability is contract/program dependent.

terms.value_text: OPEN / active. Cash balance defined-benefit plan ('Pension Plan of General Atomics'). Regular employees are eligible on their Date of Hire (temporary employees after 90 days). Entirely company-funded; no employee enrollment or contribution required. Hypothetical account balance grows annually by 7% of Eligible Compensation plus an interest credit based on the plan's Current Index. 100% vested after 3 years of Vesting Service (1,000 paid hours = 1 year). Payable as lump sum or lifetime annuity.
terms.source_snippet: Regular employees are eligible on their Date of Hire... The hypothetical Account Balance grows annually by 7% of an employee's Eligible Compensation for that year, plus an interest credit... becomes 100% vested with three years of Vesting Service.
terms.notes: Confirms the Form 5500 DB pension (~17,785 participants) is a CASH BALANCE plan that remains OPEN to new regular hires (eligible on date of hire) as of the employer's current 'Retirement Plans At A Glance' publication and the 2023 Total Rewards brochure. No public evidence of a freeze/close. Total Rewards brochure illustrates monthly benefit as % of projected annual salary per month: 5 yrs=5.1%, 10 yrs=10.7%, 15 yrs=16.9%, 20 yrs=23.7%.

terms.value_text: Company matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of Eligible Compensation, i.e. maximum matching contribution = 3% of Eligible Compensation (per detailed 'Retirement Plans At A Glance'). NOTE DISCREPANCY: 2023 Total Rewards brochure states match of 50% up to 8% of eligible compensation (max 4%).
terms.source_snippet: The company matches 50% of an employee's contributions up to 6% of Eligible Compensation (i.e., the maximum matching contribution is three percent (3%) of Eligible Compensation).
terms.notes: Two employer sources conflict. Detailed 'Retirement Plans At A Glance' PDF: 50% up to 6% (max 3%). Total Rewards brochure (dated 01-13-2023): 50% up to 8% (max 4%). Per spec, trusting the more precise/explicit employer source (the Retirement Plans PDF states the exact 3% cap) as primary; brochure figure noted for the discrepancy. Employees may contribute 1%-50% of salary (pretax/Roth/after-tax), subject to IRS limits.

terms.value_text: Company match vests gradually over 5 years (graded): year 1 = 0%, year 2 = 25%, year 3 = 50%, year 4 = 75%, year 5 = 100%. One year of Vesting Service per calendar year with at least 1,000 paid hours.
terms.source_snippet: Company matching contributions become vested incrementally over 5 years of service. Years of Vesting Service: 1=0%, 2=25%, 3=50%, 4=75%, 5=100%.
terms.notes: 5-year graded vesting confirmed by both Total Rewards brochure (explicit table) and Retirement Plans PDF ('vests gradually over five (5) years').

terms.value_text: Regular employees generally eligible to enroll after two weeks of employment; temporary employees eligible after 90 days.
terms.source_snippet: Regular employees are generally eligible to enroll in the 401(k) Plan after two weeks of employment; temporary employees are eligible after 90 days of employment.
terms.notes: No auto-enrollment provision stated in employer sources.

terms.value_text: Comprehensive Annual Leave (CAL) covers vacation/sick/personal, accrued by service (based on 40-hr week): <1 yr = 120 hrs (15 days); 1 to <4 yrs = 136 hrs (17 days); 4 to <9 yrs = 176 hrs (22 days); 9 to <19 yrs = 216 hrs (27 days); 19+ yrs = 256 hrs (32 days).
terms.source_snippet: CAL is accrued as follows (based on a 40 hour work week): Less than 1: 120 hours; 1, but less than 4: 136 hours; 4, but less than 9: 176 hours; 9, but less than 19: 216 hours; 19 or more: 256 hours.
terms.notes: value_num = entry accrual 120 hrs / 8 = 15 days. Single combined bucket (no separate sick). Prorated for part-time.

terms.value_text: 80 hours of paid holidays for full-time employees (= 10 days).
terms.source_snippet: Holidays (80 hours for full-time)
terms.notes: 80 hrs / 8 = 10 days.

terms.value_text: Tuition, books and related fees reimbursed for approved courses at accredited institutions. Max per year: $5,250 undergraduate, $10,000 graduate. Amount over $5,250 treated as taxable income per IRS rules.
terms.source_snippet: The maximum amount of reimbursement per year is $5,250 for undergraduate level courses and $10,000 for graduate level courses.
terms.notes: value_num uses undergraduate cap ($5,250, the IRS tax-free limit); graduate cap is $10,000.

terms.value_text: 9/80 compressed schedule offered: 80 hours over two weeks worked in 9 days (eight 9-hour days + one 8-hour day), resulting in every other Friday off.
terms.source_snippet: The Company offers the 9/80 schedule, which compresses 80 hours of work over a two-week period from 10 workdays into 9 workdays... every other Friday being a day off.
terms.notes: Other schedules may apply based on business needs.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic life insurance and AD&D at 1x base salary. Employees may purchase additional coverage at group rates.
terms.source_snippet: The Company provides eligible employees 1 x base salary in life insurance and AD&D insurance.
terms.notes: Business travel accident insurance separately provided at 3x base salary to a $300,000 max.

terms.value_text: Corporate salaried safe-harbor (401(k) QACA Plan): 100% of the first 4% of eligible comp + 50% of the next 2% = 5% effective max match (on a 6% deferral). Match formula VARIES BY BUSINESS UNIT. GD Mission Systems (GDMS) business unit publishes a richer formula: $1-for-$1 up to the first 6% of eligible pay (6% max match).
terms.source_snippet: The Plan has an Employer matching contribution of 100% of the first 4% of eligible compensation contributed to the Plan, and 50% of the next 2% of eligible compensation contributed to the Plan.
terms.notes: value_num=5 reflects the corporate-wide 401(k) QACA (safe-harbor) Plan effective max match. The GDMS business-unit benefits summary states '$1 for $1 up to the first 6% of your eligible pay' (6% max) with immediate 100% vesting — https://gdmissionsystems.com/-/media/general-dynamics/careers-redesign/benefits/2025-gdms-benefits-summary.ashx . The 401(k) Plan for Represented Employees 11-K states 'The Employer matching contribution varies based on the business unit.' So actual match ranges ~5-6% depending on business unit/plan.

terms.value_text: Employer discretionary (non-elective) contribution based on a percentage of eligible compensation, provided to participants in business units that do NOT accrue credited service under a Company-sponsored defined benefit pension plan (i.e. the 401(k) contribution substitutes for a DB pension). Percentage not disclosed in the 11-K.
terms.source_snippet: business units that do not accrue credited service under a Company-sponsored defined benefit pension plan may be eligible to receive an Employer discretionary contribution based on a percentage of their eligible compensation.
terms.notes: Discretionary; exact percentage not published. 'No Employer discretionary contributions were made to the plan for 2025 plan year' per the QACA 11-K (a $1.69M receivable was recorded for the prior year). Effective Jan 1, 2025 recipients generally must be employed on the last day of the plan year (see k401_last_day_rule).

terms.value_text: Safe-harbor employer matching contributions: immediate (100%) for participants originally hired before Jan 1, 2020; participants hired on/after Jan 1, 2020 are 100% vested after two years of continuous service. Non-safe-harbor matching and Employer discretionary contributions: three-year cliff vesting. GDMS business unit summary states matching contributions are immediately 100% vested.
terms.source_snippet: Participants hired on or after January 1, 2020, are 100% vested in safe harbor employer matching contributions ... after two years of continuous service. Non-safe harbor matching contributions and employer discretionary contributions ... are subject to a three-year cliff vesting schedule.
terms.notes: value_num=2 = years to fully vest in safe-harbor match for post-2020 hires. Pre-2020 hires: immediate (0). Discretionary/non-safe-harbor: 3-year cliff. Represented-employee plan 11-K: vesting 'varies by business unit but does not exceed three years.'

terms.value_text: Eligible to participate in the 401(k) Plan upon hire (or after reaching age 18, whichever is later). GDMS summary: eligible for most benefits on first day of employment. Automatic enrollment applies to new employees of certain business units.
terms.source_snippet: Employees are eligible to participate in the Plan upon hire or after reaching age 18, whichever is later.
terms.notes: No waiting period for deferrals/match (0 days). Represented plan has automatic enrollment for certain business units.

terms.value_text: Applies to the Employer DISCRETIONARY (non-elective) contribution only, not the safe-harbor match: effective Jan 1, 2025, participants eligible for the discretionary contribution generally must be employed on the last day of the plan year to receive it. The safe-harbor match itself has no last-day requirement.
terms.source_snippet: Effective January 1, 2025, participants eligible for the Employer discretionary contribution generally must be employed on the last day of the plan year to be to receive the Employer discretionary contribution.
terms.notes: value_num=1 flags a last-day rule, but it governs only the discretionary employer contribution; the regular safe-harbor match is not subject to it.

terms.value_text: General Dynamics still sponsors Company-sponsored defined-benefit pension plans and some populations continue to accrue credited service under them (open for certain business units, notably represented/union employees such as Electric Boat/BIW and certain legacy salaried groups). Employees in business units that do NOT accrue DB pension credited service instead receive a 401(k) Employer discretionary contribution. GD funds DB plans at scale (contributed ~$470M in 2020).
terms.source_snippet: business units that do not accrue credited service under a Company-sponsored defined benefit pension plan may be eligible to receive an Employer discretionary contribution
terms.notes: The two-tier structure (DB pension for some populations; 401(k) discretionary contribution for others) is confirmed by the 11-K language. Most salaried new hires no longer accrue a DB benefit; DB accrual persists mainly for certain represented/legacy groups. Exact per-BU open/frozen status not enumerated in public sources checked (mark specifics inferred).

terms.value_text: GDMS offers 80 hours of holiday time a year, including floating holidays (= 10 days).
terms.source_snippet: GDMS offers 80 hours of holiday time a year, including floating holidays.
terms.notes: 80 hours / 8 = 10 days. GD Mission Systems business unit figure; may vary by business unit.

terms.value_text: GDMS offers new parents six weeks of paid leave when adding to your family through birth or adoption.
terms.source_snippet: GDMS offers new parents six weeks of paid leave when adding to your family through birth or adoption.
terms.notes: GD Mission Systems business unit; paid parental leave for birth or adoption.

terms.value_text: Short Term Disability (STD) plan provides up to 26 weeks of income replacement at no cost to the employee.
terms.source_snippet: The Short Term Disability (STD) plan provides up to 26 weeks of income replacement at no cost to you.
terms.notes: GDMS. LTD kicks in beyond 26 weeks at 50% of base monthly earnings (company-provided, with buy-up option).

terms.value_text: Mission Systems provides free basic life insurance coverage equal to one times your base salary (plus free AD&D equal to 1x base salary). Supplemental life/AD&D available for purchase.
terms.source_snippet: Mission Systems provides free basic life insurance coverage equal to one times your base salary.
terms.notes: Company-paid basic life = 1x base salary; company-paid AD&D also 1x base salary. GDMS business unit.

terms.value_text: Reported employee figures: up to $4,000/calendar year for associate/bachelor coursework and up to $3,000/calendar year for master's/professional development. GD business units offer tuition assistance/reimbursement; exact cap varies by business unit.
terms.source_snippet: limit of $4,000 reimbursement in a calendar year ... Master's degree or ... professional development ... limit of $3,000 reimbursement in a calendar year.
terms.notes: Third-party/employee-reported (Indeed/Glassdoor), not an employer-published cap — marked inferred. Caps differ by business unit; the GDMS summary lists Tuition Reimbursement as a benefit but does not publish a dollar cap.

terms.value_text: GDMS states it offers flexible work schedules (depending on business needs and management approval). 9/80 compressed schedules are common at GD engineering/manufacturing sites but the GDMS summary does not name '9/80' explicitly.
terms.source_snippet: we also offer flexible work schedules (depending upon business needs and management approval).
terms.notes: Flexible schedule is confirmed; specific 9/80 program not explicitly stated in the employer summary, so marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Honeywell matches 87.5% of the first 8% of eligible pay contributed (max company match = 7% of eligible pay). Match contributed annually in a lump sum by end of January following the plan year.
terms.source_snippet: Honeywell matches 87.5% of the first 8% of eligible pay you contribute. Match will be contributed annually in a lump sum by the end of the January following the calendar year in which you contribute.
terms.notes: 87.5% x 8% = 7% of eligible pay effective max. SEC Form 11-K corroborates: 'match 87.5 percent up to the first 8 percent of base pay.'

terms.value_text: Employer matching contributions become fully (100%) vested after completion of three years of vesting service. Employee's own contributions always 100% vested. Also 100% vested at age 65, or on retirement/disability/death/RIF-layoff/divestiture.
terms.source_snippet: Honeywell matching contributions generally become fully vested after completion of three years of vesting service.
terms.notes: Cliff-style: no match vests before 3 years; 100% at 3 years. Corroborated by SEC 11-K and retirement guide.

terms.value_text: May commence participation as soon as administratively possible following hire date (generally within two weeks). Must make an affirmative election to participate; not auto-enrolled.
terms.source_snippet: You may commence participation in the plan as soon as administratively possible following your hire date (generally within two weeks). You must make an affirmative election to commence participation.
terms.notes: Effectively immediate eligibility (~within 2 weeks). No automatic enrollment; affirmative election required.

terms.value_text: To receive the annual company match for a given year, participant must remain actively employed by Honeywell through and including December 15 of that year.
terms.source_snippet: To be eligible for any match for a given year, participants generally need to remain actively employed by Honeywell through and including December 15 of that year.
terms.notes: Employment rule tied to Dec 15 rather than the exact last day of the plan year. Corroborated by US Honeywell Retirement Guide FAQ.

terms.value_text: Closed/frozen to new hires. 'No new or rehired employees participate in a Honeywell defined benefit pension plan for future service or earnings under any pension formula.' Legacy DB pension exists for prior participants; replaced by 401(k) for current/new employees.
terms.source_snippet: No new or rehired employees participate in a Honeywell defined benefit pension plan for future service or earnings under any pension formula
terms.notes: Rehires get vesting/early-retirement service credit only. Frozen legacy plan holds ~$25B assets (AlliedSignal + legacy Honeywell).

terms.value_text: Honeywell contributes $200 to the HSA for enrolled employees with annual salary up to $50,000 who are eligible to contribute. No employer HSA contribution stated for salaries above $50,000.
terms.source_snippet: Honeywell will contribute $200 for all enrolled employees with an annual salary of up to $50,000 who are eligible to contribute to an HSA
terms.notes: Flat $200, income-capped at <=$50k salary. Employer HSA contributions available only to Futureshapers enrolled in the Honeywell Medical Plan; not FM&T salaried/hourly. 2026 IRS max contributions: $4,400 single / $8,750 family (+$1,000 age 55+ catch-up).

terms.value_text: Flexible/uncapped vacation for exempt employees: scheduled with manager approval based on business/personal needs, without pre-set maximums (does not apply to CA employees hired before Jan 1, 2017, or to FM&T employees).
terms.source_snippet: Vacation time can be scheduled with manager approval based on business priorities and personal needs, without pre-set maximums
terms.notes: No fixed day count for exempt (flexible PTO); exempt employees do not accrue vacation. Non-exempt employees accrue under a separate policy.

terms.value_text: 12 days off for holidays at all U.S. sites (seven standard holidays plus five set by the SBG/site).
terms.source_snippet: All U.S. sites will have 12 days off for holidays (seven standard and five set by the SBG or site)
terms.notes: Excludes FM&T LLC employees.

terms.value_text: Eight weeks (40 business days) of paid parental leave to bond, without using vacation days. Can be taken consecutively or intermittently in 10-business-day (2-week) increments, within 12 months of birth/adoption/foster placement.
terms.source_snippet: Eight weeks (40 business days) of paid leave to focus on family without having to take vacation days.
terms.notes: Runs consecutively with short-term disability leave for birth mothers (so birth mothers get STD + 8 weeks parental).

terms.value_text: Educational assistance: up to $5,250/year for undergraduate degree programs and $7,500/year for graduate degree programs. Applicable to engineering, technology, and business-related programs at accredited institutions. Part-time employees receive 50% of maximum.
terms.source_snippet: up to $5,250 per year for undergraduate degree programs and $7,500 for graduate degree programs
terms.notes: Undergrad cap $5,250 (IRS tax-free limit); graduate cap $7,500. Grade of B or better required; requests within 60 days of completion.

terms.value_text: Short-term disability: 100% of annual base pay through 8 weeks, then 70% of base pay for weeks 9-26 (minus state disability where applicable), up to 26 weeks for non-work-related disability. 100% company-paid.
terms.source_snippet: STD: 100% of annual base pay through 8 weeks and 70% of annual base pay for weeks 9-26 ... up to 26 weeks.
terms.notes: Also 56 hours paid sick leave per calendar year (more in some locations per local law).

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic life insurance = 1x annual base pay (100% company paid). Optional Group Universal Life up to 8x base pay (employee-paid); AD&D 1.5x base pay company-paid; business travel accident up to 5x base pay company-paid.
terms.source_snippet: BASIC LIFE INSURANCE - 1 times annual base pay - Cost of Coverage - 100% Company Paid
terms.notes: Voluntary GUL up to 8x self / 4x spouse is employee-paid.

terms.value_text: Tiered match on the HII Savings Plan (401k): 100% on the first 2% of eligible compensation, 50% on the next 2%, and 25% on the next 4%. An employee deferring 8% receives the full employer match of 4% of eligible compensation.
terms.source_snippet: The Company's matching contributions are generally as follows: First 2% of participant's eligible compensation 100%; Next 2% 50%; Next 4% 25%.
terms.notes: From HII Savings Plan Form 11-K (FY2022), the salaried/non-represented plan. Participants may contribute 1%-75% of eligible compensation (pre-tax, Roth, or after-tax). Catch-up contributions matched at same rate. Match may vary by business unit and is subject to certain collective bargaining agreements; represented/union shipyard populations may have different match terms.

terms.value_text: Age-based non-elective employer Retirement Account Contribution (RAC): 3% of eligible compensation if under age 35, 4% if age 35-49, 5% if age 50 or older.
terms.source_snippet: The Company credits participants who meet eligibility requirements with a RAC each pay period... Participant's Age: Less than 35 = 3%; 35-49 = 4%; 50 or older = 5%.
terms.notes: RAC replaced the salaried defined-benefit pension for employees hired/rehired on or after March 31, 2011. Effective July 1, 2021 the plan was amended to eliminate RAC for certain new hires/rehires/transferees; those eligible on June 30, 2021 remain 'grandfathered RAC participants.' value_num shown is the entry (under-35) tier; max is 5%.

terms.value_text: Employee and employer matching contributions are immediately vested. Retirement Account Contributions (RAC) are fully vested upon completion of three years of vesting service (cliff).
terms.source_snippet: Plan participants are immediately vested in their own and employer matching contributions... Plan participants are fully vested in their RAC, plus earnings thereon, upon the completion of three years of vesting service.
terms.notes: Match vests immediately (0 years); RAC has a 3-year cliff. value_num reflects the longest applicable vesting (RAC).

terms.value_text: First-time eligible employees (newly hired, rehired, or certain transfers) are automatically enrolled at a 2% pre-tax deferral, with automatic 1% annual increase if no election is made.
terms.source_snippet: First time eligible employee (newly hired, rehired or certain transfers) participants are enrolled automatically into the Plan at a 2% tax-deferred...contributions are automatically increased by 1% each year thereafter.
terms.notes: Auto-enrollment is immediate for eligible employees; auto-escalation 1%/year.

terms.value_text: Salaried defined-benefit pension closed to new hires: employees hired/rehired on or after March 31, 2011 receive a 401(k) Retirement Account Contribution (RAC) instead of a pension. Legacy defined-benefit pension plans remain in place for certain represented/union groups (e.g., the Newport News Operations Pension Plan and the Ingalls Shipbuilding Hourly Employees' Retirement Plan).
terms.source_snippet: employees hired or re-hired on or after March 31, 2011, who meet specific requirements are eligible to receive an additional employer contribution known as a Retirement Account Contribution ("RAC").
terms.notes: SEC 11-K confirms the salaried DB pension was closed to new hires as of March 31, 2011 (replaced by RAC). Union/represented shipyard populations retain legacy DB pensions per plan SPDs and third-party sources; those plans are governed by collective bargaining agreements and vary by division. Status of union plans (open vs frozen) not verified per-group.

terms.value_text: Education Assistance reimburses tuition and other costs up to $5,250 per employee per year; enrollment offered twice a year (May and November).
