terms.source_snippet: Education Assistance can reimburse tuition and other costs up to $5,250 per employee each year. Enrollment periods are offered twice a year, in May and November.
terms.notes: Third-party advisory source; $5,250 matches the IRS Section 127 tax-free maximum. HII careers page confirms 'Tuition Reimbursement' is offered but gives no dollar amount. Benefit packages vary by HII division.

terms.value_text: 100% match on the first 3% of compensation, plus 50% match on the next 2% (max employer match of 4% at a 5% employee deferral).
terms.source_snippet: entitled to receive 100% employer matching contributions for the first 3% and 50% for the next 2% of their compensation
terms.notes: From ICF International Retirement Savings Plan disclosure in the FY2021 Form 10-K. Participants may defer up to 70% of compensation subject to statutory limits.

terms.value_text: Employer matching contributions reported as immediately (100%) vested.
terms.source_snippet: The 401(k) offers immediate vesting with a company match up to 4% when contributing 5%.
terms.notes: Immediate vesting per third-party aggregator (Built In) summary; not independently confirmed against an ICF/SEC plan document.

terms.value_text: Discount not to exceed 5% of the market value on the purchase date; six-month offering periods; up to $25,000 per calendar year via payroll deduction.
terms.source_snippet: purchase shares ... over six-month offering periods at a discount not to exceed 5% of the market value on the date of each purchase period
terms.notes: ESPP terms are consistent across ICF International 10-K filings (FY2017, FY2019, FY2020). Company does not recognize compensation expense on the ESPP due to the small (<=5%) discount.

terms.value_text: None. ICF International sponsors a 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan only; no defined-benefit pension is offered to U.S. employees.
terms.source_snippet: ICF International Retirement Savings Plan ... 100% employer matching contributions for the first 3% and 50% for the next 2%
terms.notes: Only a defined-contribution 401(k) plan is referenced in ICF filings/benefits materials; no defined-benefit pension found for current employees.

terms.value_text: Paid paternity/parental bonding leave reported at 4 weeks; paid maternity leave reported at 5 weeks at 100% pay (via short-term disability), with additional weeks at 60% pay. Adoption assistance also offered.
terms.source_snippet: 5 weeks at 100% pay through short-term disability, with additional weeks available at 60% of pay ... offers 4 weeks of paid paternity leave
terms.notes: Third-party/employee-reported figures; exact current ICF policy not confirmed from an employer-published document.

terms.value_text: PTO accrues weekly and increases with tenure; entry-level accrual reported around 0-10 days per year. Up to 2 weeks carry over annually.
terms.source_snippet: ICF's PTO and Vacation policy typically gives 0-10 days a year.
terms.notes: Third-party/employee-reported; PTO is tenure-based and accrues each pay period. Entry figure is approximate.

terms.value_text: Approximately 10 paid company holidays per year.
terms.source_snippet: ICF offers 10 holidays compared to the federal holiday schedule with 11.
terms.notes: Third-party/employee-reported (Glassdoor); not confirmed from an employer-published document.

terms.value_text: Matching Contributions total 50% of each participant's pre-tax contributions, with no match on contributions above 6% of pay (i.e. max ~3% of pay). Rate is changeable by the Employer/Committee.
terms.source_snippet: The Matching Contributions for each period shall total 50% of each eligible Participant's Pre-Tax Contributions... no Matching Contributions shall be made based upon a Participant's Contributions in excess of 6% of his or her Pay.
terms.notes: From the Jacobs Engineering Group Inc. 401(k) Plus Savings Plan document filed with SEC (2007 restatement). The plan text notes the 50%/6% rates are subject to change. Third-party/Glassdoor employee reports indicate a current match of roughly 4.5% of pay; this could not be confirmed against a current employer/SEC source, so the SEC-documented formula is reported here and the discrepancy is flagged.

terms.value_text: Participants are fully vested in all accounts (including employer matching contributions) at all times - immediate vesting.
terms.source_snippet: 8.1 Fully Vested Accounts A Participant shall be fully vested in all Accounts at all times.
terms.notes: 401(k) Plus Savings Plan Section 8.1. Immediate/full vesting of employer contributions.

terms.value_text: For pre-tax contributions, an eligible employee becomes a participant as soon as administratively feasible after date of hire but not more than 30 days. Matching eligibility begins after a 12-month period with at least 1,000 Hours of Service.
terms.source_snippet: Each other Eligible Employee shall become a Participant as soon as administratively feasible after date of hire but not more than 30 days. For purposes of Matching Contributions... first day of the next month after... 12-month eligibility period... at least 1,000 Hours of Service.
terms.notes: 401(k) Plus Savings Plan Section 2.1. 30-day cap for deferral eligibility; separate 12-month/1,000-hour service requirement before matching contributions begin.

terms.value_text: ESPP purchase price equals 95% of the Closing Value of a share on the last trading day of the purchase period (5% discount). Committee may set the price anywhere from 85% to 100%; currently 95%.
terms.source_snippet: "Purchase Price" shall mean, for each Share purchased... an amount equal to ninety-five percent (95%) of the Closing Value of a Share on the last Trading Day in a Purchase Period.
terms.notes: Jacobs Engineering Group Inc. Global Employee Stock Purchase Plan (SEC exhibit). No look-back beyond last day per this provision; committee has discretion within an 85%-100% band.

terms.value_text: Defined-benefit pension is frozen to new benefit accruals and closed to new hires; Jacobs relies on the 401(k) Plus Savings Plan for new employees. Accrued benefits for previously vested participants are preserved.
terms.source_snippet: Jacobs Engineering Group has frozen its defined benefit pension to new accruals... new employees hired after the freeze date would not accrue additional pension benefits through this plan.
terms.notes: Third-party advisor source; Jacobs Engineering Group Pension Plan still exists as a legacy frozen DB plan. Not offered to current/new hires.

terms.value_text: Paid parental leave reported as up to 16 weeks for birthing/primary-caregiver mothers and up to 8 weeks for fathers/non-birthing parents (some sources cite ~6 weeks). Jacobs markets 'competitive parental leave'.
terms.source_snippet: up to 16 weeks of paid leave for mothers and up to 8 weeks of paid leave for fathers
terms.notes: Third-party figures vary (Glassdoor cites up to 6 weeks; JobzMall cites 16 weeks mothers / 8 weeks fathers). value_num set to the non-birthing/general parental figure (8). No employer-published week count confirmed; benefits vary by business unit/contract.

terms.value_text: Tuition assistance / reimbursement for higher education is offered; no published annual dollar cap located.
terms.source_snippet: Provides tuition assistance
terms.notes: Existence confirmed by aggregator; specific annual cap not published in a citable employer source.

terms.value_text: Health Savings Account (HSA) offered with medical plan; employer contribution amount not published in a citable source.
terms.source_snippet: Offers Health Savings Account (HSA)
terms.notes: HSA existence confirmed by aggregator; employer seed/match amount not disclosed in public sources reviewed.

terms.value_text: $2 for every $1 of staff member contributions, up to a maximum APL contribution of 8 percent of pay (so a ~4% employee deferral captures the full 8% match).
terms.source_snippet: plus matching contributions of $2 for every $1 of staff member contributions, up to a maximum APL contribution of 8 percent of pay.
terms.notes: Plan is a 403(b) defined-contribution plan (recorded under 401k vocabulary). Total employer contribution up to 10.5% of pay = 2.5% foundational + up to 8% match. 2-for-1 match rate is unusually generous.

terms.value_text: APL foundational (non-elective) contribution equal to 2.5 percent of pay, independent of staff contributions.
terms.source_snippet: This includes an APL contribution equal to 2.5 percent of pay, plus matching contributions of $2 for every $1 of staff member contributions
terms.notes: Part of the up-to-10.5% total employer retirement contribution.

terms.value_text: Immediately 100 percent vested in all Laboratory contributions.
terms.source_snippet: Staff members ... are immediately 100 percent vested in all Laboratory contributions.
terms.notes: No vesting schedule; full and immediate vesting of employer money.

terms.value_text: Staff members become eligible for APL's contributions after one year of employment (careers page: contributions commence after 12 months of service).
terms.source_snippet: Staff members become eligible for APL's contributions after one year of employment
terms.notes: Careers page corroborates: 'APL's contributions commence after 12 months of service.' Employee can defer earlier; employer money starts at 1 year.

terms.value_text: No traditional defined-benefit pension identified. APL's retirement benefit is a 403(b) defined-contribution plan (recordkept at Vanguard; branded 'Pension Plan B'). Employer funds up to 10.5% of pay.
terms.source_snippet: jhu applied physics laboratory - pension plan b - Vanguard
terms.notes: The 'Pension Plan B' naming refers to a defined-contribution (403(b)) account at Vanguard, not an open DB pension. No employer-published evidence of an accruing defined-benefit pension for new hires.

terms.value_text: Entry vacation = 15 days/year. Matrix (Full Time): Supporting & Associate staff: 1-10 yrs = 15, 11-19 yrs = 20, 20+ yrs = 25. Senior staff: 1-5 yrs = 15, 6-15 yrs = 20, 16+ yrs = 25. Plus up to 7 sick days and 1 personal day per year.
terms.source_snippet: Paid vacation is based on years of employment and staff level. ... Supporting and Associate 1-10=15, 11-19=20, 20+=25; Senior 1-5=15, 6-15=20, 16+=25
terms.notes: value_num uses entry-level vacation (15). Sick (up to 7) and personal (1) are separate from vacation.

terms.value_text: APL pays tuition for up to 24 credit hours per academic year for a relevant degree or part-time coursework. Part-time doctoral students in dissertation phase get 200 hours of paid leave/year for up to 3 years.
terms.source_snippet: The Lab will pay tuition for up to 24 credit hours per academic year for a relevant degree or part-time coursework.
terms.notes: Cap is expressed as credit hours, not a dollar amount; no published USD cap. Dependent college scholarships (50% of tuition/fees) available after 5 years of employment, awarded competitively.

terms.value_text: APL offers '80-Hour Flex Scheduling' (80 hours per two-week pay period) and some teams support compressed workweeks; some positions allow schedule adjustment and remote work.
terms.source_snippet: 80-Hour Flex Scheduling - 80 hours per two-week pay period ... some teams support compressed weeks
terms.notes: Employer PDF states 'Some positions offer the flexibility of adjusting your work schedule.' A formal named '9/80' program is not explicitly published; captured as flexible/compressed scheduling.

terms.value_text: Reported as up to 80 hours (approx. 2 weeks) of paid parental leave for birth/adoption per third-party aggregators; not detailed on the employer benefits PDF.
terms.source_snippet: up to 80 hours of paid parental leave for births and adoptions
terms.notes: Third-party lead only; APL's published benefits materials do not state a specific paid-parental-leave duration. Additional unpaid FMLA leave available.

terms.value_text: 100% match on the first 6% of pretax and Roth contribution elections. Match increased to 6% in 2025 (legacy formula was $1/$1 up to 3% plus $0.50/$1 on next 3% = 4.5% total).
terms.source_snippet: KBR offers a 401k retirement plan with 100% match on 6% of pretax and Roth contribution elections.
terms.notes: Current 6% match reported across multiple third-party sources for 2025; not verified against an employer total-rewards PDF or latest 11-K. Historical SEC 11-K documented a tiered 4.5% match.

terms.value_text: Employer matching contributions vest after a three-year period (cliff).
terms.source_snippet: Employees become vested in matching contributions after a three-year period; three or more years of credited service = fully vested.
terms.notes: Three-year vesting cited from an older KBR SEC 11-K and repeated by third-party summaries; current schedule not re-verified against a recent 11-K.

terms.value_text: Eligible to participate in the KBR 401(k) Savings Plan after completing 30 days of service.
terms.source_snippet: Employees are eligible to participate in the KBR 401(k) Savings Plan after completing 30 days of service.
terms.notes: Third-party advisor source.

terms.value_text: Legacy defined-benefit pension plans exist (formula based on years of service and average salary) but are largely closed/frozen; the 401(k) Savings Plan is the primary retirement vehicle for current U.S. employees.
terms.source_snippet: KBR provides a defined benefit pension plan based on a formula that includes years of service and average salary.
terms.notes: KBR carries legacy/UK DB pension obligations; not open to new U.S. hires as a standard offering. Status inferred from third-party and 10-K disclosures, not a benefits PDF.

terms.value_text: Combined PTO bank (vacation + sick) accruing by tenure; third-party sources cite roughly 20-30 days/year, entry accrual ~11 hours/month.
terms.source_snippet: KBR's PTO and Vacation policy typically gives 20-30 days off a year; 11 hours are accrued per month.
terms.notes: value_num set to the low end of the reported range as entry/minimum; employee-reported, not employer-published.

terms.value_text: 9 paid holidays plus one floating holiday.
terms.source_snippet: There are 9 paid holidays with one floating holiday.
terms.notes: Employee-reported.

terms.value_text: ESPP allows withholding up to 10% of earnings; 5% discount on stock price at the end of each six-month offering period (Feb and Aug).
terms.source_snippet: employees who participate receive a 5% discount on the stock price at the end of each period; up to 10% of earnings.
terms.notes: Discount described via SEC 10-K summary and third-party sources; 10-K page returned 403 on direct fetch, so marked inferred.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic life insurance paying approximately two years' salary to beneficiaries; supplemental coverage available for purchase.
terms.source_snippet: offers fully paid life insurance policies... The policy pays around two years worth of salary to employees' beneficiaries.
terms.notes: Third-party/aggregator description; not confirmed against an employer benefits PDF.

terms.value_text: Company matches a portion of employee 401(k) contributions; exact match formula not publicly disclosed. Aggregate employer matching contributions to the defined-contribution plans totaled $11.8M (2024), $10.7M (2023), $8.1M (2022).
terms.source_snippet: In certain plans, the Company matches a portion of the employees' contributions. The Company's matching contributions to these defined-contribution savings plans totaled $11.8 million in 2024
terms.notes: Match existence confirmed by employer careers page and SEC 10-K (FY2024, Note 11 Retirement Plans). No per-salary match percentage/formula published; value_num left null to avoid fabrication.

terms.value_text: Eligible to participate in the 401(k) with company matching after completing one month of employment (~30 days).
terms.source_snippet: Kratos employees become eligible to participate in the 401K retirement savings plan with company matching contributions after completing one month of employment.
terms.notes: Employer careers page. One month approximated as 30 days.

terms.value_text: None. Kratos sponsors only defined-contribution 401(k) savings plans; no defined-benefit pension is described in SEC filings (Note 11, Retirement Plans).
terms.source_snippet: The Company provides eligible employees the opportunity to participate in defined-contribution savings plans (commonly known as 401(k) plans), which permit contributions on a before-tax basis.
terms.notes: FY2024 10-K Retirement Plans note describes defined-contribution plans only; no defined-benefit/pension plan mentioned.

terms.value_text: ESPP allows employees to purchase Kratos common stock at a 15% discount.
terms.source_snippet: Our Employee Stock Purchasing Program (ESPP) allows employees to purchase Kratos common stock at a 15 percent discount.
terms.notes: Employer careers page. Underlying plans (1999 Purchase Plan, 2023 Purchase Plan) referenced in FY2024 10-K.

terms.value_text: Company-paid basic Life Insurance and AD&D coverage equal to 1.5x annual salary at no cost to the employee.
terms.source_snippet: Kratos provides Life Insurance and Accidental Death and Dismemberment (AD&D) coverage equal to one and a half times annual salary at no cost to the employee.
terms.notes: Employer careers page.

terms.value_text: Tuition reimbursement offered; courses/certifications must apply to the employee's Kratos role and require prior approval. Annual cap reported at ~$10,000 by third-party/employee sources.
terms.source_snippet: Tuition reimbursement has a $10,000 maximum per year
terms.notes: Program existence confirmed on employer careers page; $10,000 cap sourced from third-party/employee reviews (not employer-published), hence inferred.

terms.value_text: PTO accrues based on length of service and level of position (manager approval to use). First-year employees reported to accrue roughly 14 days.

terms.source_snippet: All contributions are 100% vested after 3 years (25% after year 1, 50% after year 2 and 100% after year 3).
terms.notes: From V2X 2026 DEF 14A. Prior Vertex/Vectrus service counts toward the vesting schedule per the 2022 merger FAQ.

terms.value_text: none - V2X does not provide a traditional defined benefit pension plan.
terms.source_snippet: V2X has not adopted a traditional defined benefit pension plan and does not provide traditional defined benefit pension benefits to the NEOs.
terms.notes: Confirmed in the Pension Benefits section of the 2026 DEF 14A. V2X does maintain a non-qualified, unfunded Excess Savings Plan for key employees (not a DB pension).

terms.value_text: Alternate/compressed work schedules such as 4/10 and 9/80 are offered at locations that require them to meet customer requirements, subject to FLSA and state/local labor law.
terms.source_snippet: We offer alternate work schedules in locations that require it to meet customer requirements... such as the Fair Labor Standards Act (FLSA).
terms.notes: Employer-published V2X FAQ (July 2022). 9/80 availability is location/contract dependent, not company-wide, hence inferred.

terms.value_text: V2X offers a Tuition Reimbursement / Tuition Assistance program (tuition and/or certification reimbursement); no public annual dollar cap found.
terms.source_snippet: There are no changes to the Tuition Reimbursement Program in 2022.
terms.notes: Program existence confirmed by employer FAQ and careers page (gov2x.com / Built In 'Provides tuition assistance'). Specific annual cap not publicly disclosed; not confirmed to be the IRS $5,250 threshold.

terms.value_text: V2X offers paid parental leave ('generous parental leave' per careers/Built In); number of weeks not publicly disclosed.
terms.source_snippet: Offers generous parental leave
terms.notes: Existence confirmed by employer careers page and Built In; leave plan referenced in 2022 merger FAQ. Duration in weeks not found in any public source.

terms.value_text: V2X offers a Health Savings Account (HSA) with an HDHP; employer HSA contribution amount not publicly disclosed.
terms.source_snippet: Offers Health Savings Account (HSA)
terms.notes: HSA availability confirmed via Built In and careers page. No public benefits-guide PDF found stating the employer seed/match dollar amount or tier/salary matrix.

terms.value_text: PTO accrued biweekly (employee-reported ~6.67 hours per 2-week period, roughly 17-18 days/year for entry-level exempt staff); varies by tenure, location and CBA.
terms.source_snippet: Employees receive approximately 6.67 hours of PTO every 2 weeks (~17-18 days per year).
terms.notes: Third-party (Glassdoor/Indeed) employee reports; no verbatim employer figure. Estimate only; actual PTO varies by legacy plan (Vertex/Vectrus/TTS), service length, and collective bargaining agreements.
